track_prompt: Use the experiment-loop skill. Create a polished, reusable SVG event-card design system for a fictional "Loop Lab" workshop. The cards must communicate event title, date, time, venue, and call to action at a glance while remaining reusable across event variants. Score visual hierarchy, brand distinctiveness, information clarity, system coherence, production polish, SVG validity, and layout quality. Subjective criteria use a 1-5 point scale and maximize. SVG validity and layout quality use pass/fail objective gates: overlap, clipping, unreadable text, inaccessible contrast, missing required content, or invalid SVG prevents Champion promotion. Visual hierarchy is the primary criterion. [Editorial-typography Track scope: use claude-sonnet-5, at least two Loops, strong typographic rhythm, restrained geometric accents, clear reading order, print-grade spacing.]
input_feedback: No prior loop exists. This is the track's first build; there is no baseline artifact or judge note to react to yet. Design intent for this loop: establish a print-inspired editorial grid (kicker + rule lines + three-column metadata + footer CTA) as the reusable skeleton for the whole track, then measure it honestly before polishing.
judge_feedback: Self-measured via layout-metrics.json (no external judge panel is in scope for this track deliverable). Findings: visual hierarchy is strong (large serif title dominates, metadata is clearly secondary, CTA is isolated bottom-right with its own filled surface) and the grid produces genuine editorial rhythm (kicker, rule lines, column dividers, footer microcopy echoing a masthead). All geometric gates pass: no overlap across 19 checked adjacent-element pairs, no clipping against the 1200x630 canvas, all five required content types present, minimum font size 16px. However the objective contrast check fails: the ink.muted token (#A39C8C) used for the three metadata labels, the edition tag, and the microcopy line measures 2.49:1 against the paper background (#F8F4EC), well under the 4.5:1 AA threshold for normal text and even under the 3:1 large-text threshold. This is a hard accessible-contrast gate failure and blocks this loop from being a clean pass, even though no other criterion is compromised.
next_prompt: Keep the loop-01 grid, type scale, and component structure intact (they satisfy hierarchy, coherence, and geometry) but repair the ink.muted color token so every caption/label text node clears WCAG AA (>=4.5:1 on the paper background), and re-verify all five affected text nodes plus the full overlap/clipping/content gate suite so nothing regresses. Additionally, extend the token set to demonstrate reuse: add at least one event variant (e.g. a virtual/hybrid session) or an explicit variant/extensibility mechanism in tokens.json, proving the system generalizes beyond a single hardcoded event.
